conversation.content: Let's learn about a law. Today we are going to understand the Ratchet Trade Reputation Protection Law in the world of Azeroth. Unlike regular commercial laws, its restrictions on reputation and speech are very strict.
My notebook is ready, let's begin.

The core rule of this law is: those who publicly defame trade agreements will be fined 100 gold coins, required to post a letter of repentance at the tavern door for three days, and simultaneously prohibited from engaging in trading activities within the Chamber of Commerce's jurisdiction for six months; if the defamatory content spreads widely, the fine will be increased to 500 gold coins and they will be expelled from Ratchet.
I have written down the core penalty standards of this law.
Its application scenario is: all taverns and Chamber of Commerce premises in Ratchet.
Okay, I understand the application scenario of this law.
Let's test it. If a merchant grossly defames a new trade agreement in a tavern, causing half of Ratchet to spread rumors, what penalty should he face according to the rules?
Because the defamatory content spread widely, the merchant will be fined 500 gold coins and directly expelled from Ratchet.
Completely correct, you have mastered the sentencing standards of this law.
I will firmly remember this Ratchet Trade Reputation Protection Law and its application method.
